kind.prompt:
Fix the following AOT-compatibility IL warnings in the project at `/tmp/sv-3a61387c53ee4c3aa01b8e113c446a1d/before`. Make ONLY the changes specified below. Do NOT modify any other files.

## 1. ArmOperation.cs — Fix IL2087/IL2090

Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` annotations to `T` on the methods that use reflection. The method `IsResource<T>()` uses `typeof(T).GetConstructor(...)` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`, which requires NonPublicConstructors | PublicConstructors.

Changes needed:
- Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not already present
- On `Rehydrate<T>` method (line ~44): change `T` constraint to include `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]`
- On `RehydrateAsync<T>` method (line ~82): same annotation on `T`
- On `IsResource<T>` method (line ~96): same annotation on `T`

The syntax is:
```csharp
public static ArmOperation<T> Rehydrate<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>(...)
```

## 2. GenericOperationSource.cs — Fix IL2087

The class `GenericOperationSource<T>` uses `typeof(T)` in `ModelReaderWriter.Read` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`.

Changes needed:
- On the class declaration (line ~16), add the annotation to `T`:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

## 3. ArmEnvironment.cs — Fix IL2026/IL3050 at line 80

Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(this)` in `ToString()` with manual JSON string building. The `ArmEnvironment` struct has `Endpoint` (Uri) and `Audience` (string) properties.

Change line 80 from:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
to:
```csharp
public override string ToString()
{
    return $"{{\"Endpoint\":\"{Endpoint}\",\"Audience\":\"{Audience}\"}}";
}
```

Also remove `using System.Text.Json;` if it's no longer needed (check if any other code in the file uses it — it's unlikely). If removing the using causes compilation issues, keep it.

## 4. ArmClientOptions.cs — Fix IL2026/IL3050 at line 52

Replace `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span)` with `JsonDocument.Parse` and manual element access.

Change the method `SetApiVersionsFromProfile` to use `JsonDocument.Parse` instead of `JsonSerializer.Deserialize`:

Before (around line 51-52):
```csharp
var span = BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span;
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
var armProfile = allProfile["resource-manager"];
```

After:
```csharp
var data = BinaryData.FromStream(stream);
using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(data);
var armProfile = profileDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("resource-manager");
```

Then the iteration below needs to change from `Dictionary` iteration to `JsonElement` iteration:
- `foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile)` → `foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile.EnumerateObject())`
- `var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Key;` → `var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Name;`
- `var element = keyValuePair.Value;` stays the same (JsonProperty.Value returns JsonElement)
- `foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())` stays the same since it was already using JsonElement

Wait, the original code has `var element = keyValuePair.Value;` where `keyValuePair.Value` was a `Dictionary<string, JsonElement>`. Now `keyValuePair` is a `JsonProperty`, so `keyValuePair.Value` is a `JsonElement`. The inner loop `element.EnumerateObject()` should work the same.

Also remove `using System.Text.Json;` only if no longer needed. Actually, `JsonElement` and `JsonDocument` are in `System.Text.Json`, so keep the using.

But we do need to remove or keep `using System.Text.Json` — keep it since `JsonDocument`, `JsonElement` etc. are still used.

## 5. ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs — Fix IL2026/IL3050 at line 30

The method `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` calls `builder.RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)` which is marked with `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode]`.

Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` and propagate the attributes:

```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder source generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types. Use the Configuration Binder source generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
public static IAzureClientBuilder<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions> AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>(this TBuilder builder, TConfiguration configuration)
```

## 6. LRO files (4 files) — Fix IL2026/IL3050 at line 61

All 4 LRO files have the same pattern. Replace `.ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>()` with `JsonDocument.Parse` and `.GetProperty("id").GetString()`:

Files:
- `/tmp/sv-3a61387c53ee4c3aa01b8e113c446a1d/before/ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs`
- `/tmp/sv-3a61387c53ee4c3aa01b8e113c446a1d/before/ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`
- `/tmp/sv-3a61387c53ee4c3aa01b8e113c446a1d/before/Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs`
- `/tmp/sv-3a61387c53ee4c3aa01b8e113c446a1d/before/Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`

In each file, change the `GetOperationId` method's body from:
```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
return lroDetails["id"];
```
to:
```csharp
var lroData = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default);
using var lroDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(lroData);
return lroDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("id").GetString();
```

Also add `using System.Text.Json;` to each file if not already present.

IMPORTANT: Do not change any other files. Only edit the files listed above. Make the minimal changes necessary.
